title: subagent: model_override resolved twice and ignored when run_in_background spawns internally
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
dry
## Location
src/subagent.rs:1283-1331 (execute_task_tool) and src/subagent.rs:753-763 (run_subagent model resolution)
## Violation
`execute_task_tool` resolves the model name via `resolve_model_name` (line 1289-1292) and stores it on `SubagentConfig::model_override`. `run_subagent` then resolves model again, this time consulting `AgentType::preferred_model` and provider config defaults (lines 753-763). The two code paths can produce different model strings:

- `execute_task_tool` will pass `"opus" -> "claude-opus-4-6"` through `resolve_model_name`.
- `run_subagent`'s fallback chain uses `provider.model` raw.

So a friendly name typed by the caller is canonicalized, but a default from provider config is not — they end up as different string formats downstream.

Additionally, the background-spawn branch (lines 1316-1331) clones the entire `app_config` into the spawned task per call. For a config with many providers, hooks, and rules, this is megabytes of copy. `app_config: Arc<AppConfig>` would let the spawn share the reference.

## Mandated refactor
Move the friendly-name resolution into `run_subagent::pick_model` so both paths normalize identically. Switch `SubagentConfig::model_override` from `Option<String>` to `Option<&str>` consumed only in pick_model. Make `AppConfig` arc-shared so background spawn just clones the Arc.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
